tools.name: search
tools.description: Search TapWaterMap for US cities by name. Returns matching cities with an id you can pass to `fetch`. (ChatGPT/Deep-Research compatible.)

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch the full plain-English EPA tap-water record for a city id (e.g. 'vt/burlington') returned by `search`. (ChatGPT/Deep-Research compatible.)

tools.name: search_cities
tools.description: Find covered US cities by name, optionally within a state. Returns counts + the page URL for each.

tools.name: get_city_water
tools.description: The core tool: what the EPA has on record for a city's tap water — the active community water systems serving it, populations, sources, and violations (contaminant, health-based or not, dates, status, level vs limit when recorded). Plain-English, sourced, never a safety verdict.

tools.name: get_water_system
tools.description: EPA record for a single water system by its PWSID (e.g. 'VT0005053'): name, population served, source, and violations.

tools.name: list_state_cities
tools.description: List all covered cities in a state (with violation counts).

tools.name: find_cities
tools.description: Filter/rank cities across the dataset — e.g. cities in TX with open health-based violations, or cities with a specific contaminant. Returns ranked matches.

tools.name: compare_cities
tools.description: Compare the EPA records of several cities side by side. Pass cities as 'City, ST' strings.

tools.name: explain_contaminant
tools.description: Plain-English explanation of an EPA contaminant by name or code: the EPA name, whether the EPA classifies it as health-based, and how often it appears in our records. Not medical advice.

tools.name: coverage_stats
tools.description: What TapWaterMap covers: states, cities, total EPA violation records, and the data quarter/date.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.
